topic: Fabric-v2 核心认知对齐方案架构分析与实施准备
summary: All pre-TDD protocol clarifications are locked. selection_token uses in-memory cache + TTL; invalid L1 selection is hard error; missing AI selection reasons is hard error; rule_selection audit goes to .fabric/audit.jsonl; fab_plan_context may return neutral requirement_profile; description_index uses minimal schema without scoring/reason fields; missing sections return empty content plus warning diagnostic with no full-content fallback.

key_conclusions.point: L1 must be domain/module based, not path-depth derived.
key_conclusions.evidence: packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts derives layer from path depth; packages/cli/src/commands/sync-meta.ts scans .fabric/agents paths.

key_conclusions.point: The current two-stage discovery model exists only as description stubs and needs Requirement Profile ranking.
key_conclusions.evidence: packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts exposes description_stub_union but no score or profile.

key_conclusions.point: Structured MD injection needs explicit section parsing and a batch retrieval API.
key_conclusions.evidence: packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts reads entire rule files today.

key_conclusions.point: Implementation should pause until TDD preflight decisions are confirmed.
Compatibility with .fabric/agents is no longer a requirement for the refactor.
Requirement Profile should be deterministic and server-generated, using path, intent, tech, impact, and detected entity hints for transparent ranking.
fab_plan_context should return a unified L0/L1/L2 description index while limiting AI selection to L1.
fab_get_rule_sections should use selection_token to merge required L0/L2 ids with AI-selected L1 ids server-side.
L1 selection reasons must be structured protocol fields, not informal assistant prose.
Rule selection telemetry should record required ids, candidate ids, AI-selected ids, final ids, reasons, and rejected/ignored ids.
fab_plan_context should not expose L1 score, confidence, match_reasons, negative_reasons, or matched_profile_fields in normal output.
L1 selection reasons belong to fab_get_rule_sections input and audit telemetry, not plan-context candidate output.
RuleDescription must not include id; stable_id is the only rule identity.
TDD entry decisions are now locked: token cache/TTL, hard errors, audit destination, neutral profile, minimal index, and missing-section behavior.

recommendations.rationale: Initial L1 bucket decisions must be durable and reviewable, otherwise later agents cannot know why a domain boundary exists.

steps.verification: Init tests assert the file path, action, and generated content.

steps.description: Add the three-tier constitution to bootstrap guide generation.
steps.verification: Bootstrap content contains L0/L1/L2 definitions and timing contract.
Docs describe INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md and its evolution role.

recommendations.rationale: The proposed L1 domain model conflicts with current shadow-mirroring path-depth derivation.

steps.description: Extend AgentsMetaNode with explicit domain/topology/content_ref fields.
steps.verification: Shared schema tests accept explicit registry nodes.

steps.description: Keep legacy .fabric/agents importer as compatibility path.
steps.verification: Existing shadow-mirroring fixture tests still pass.

recommendations.rationale: A string description cannot support reliable L1 intent matching or explainability.

steps.description: Introduce RuleDescription schema with summary, intent_clues, tech_stack, impact, must_read_if, and entities.
steps.verification: Schema tests parse both structured and legacy descriptions.

recommendations.action: Add Requirement Profile ranking to fab_plan_context
recommendations.rationale: Planning should return candidate descriptions and match reasons before full rule injection.

steps.description: Accept optional user intent, known tech, and detected entities in plan-context input.
Return ranked_candidates with score and match_reasons.
steps.verification: Service tests cover L1 profile ranking.

recommendations.rationale: Editing should inject mandatory blocks after concrete script inspection without loading every section.

steps.description: Parse section markers such as [MANDATORY_INJECTION] and [CONTEXT_INFO].
steps.verification: Parser tests cover missing, repeated, and ordered sections.

steps.description: Expose a batch API/tool to fetch selected sections by stable_id.
steps.verification: Tool tests return only requested mandatory sections.

recommendations.action: Encode L2 over L1 over L0 conflict precedence
recommendations.rationale: Conflict resolution must be protocol-visible and testable, not only described in prompts.

steps.description: Add effective_order or precedence metadata to rules payload.
steps.verification: Tests prove deterministic L0/L1/L2 ordering and override documentation.

intent_coverage.notes: Accepted and made explicit in schema/precedence.

intent_coverage.intent: Drop shadow physical directories and centralize mapping in agents.meta.json
intent_coverage.notes: Use registry-first plus content_ref rather than JSON-only rule content.
Add ranking and match reasons to fab_plan_context.

findings_coverage.finding: Description is currently a plain string.
plan-context has stubs but no ranking.
full MD content is loaded rather than sections.

decision_trail.decision: Pause direct implementation and convert the plan into TDD preflight phases.
decision_trail.context: User asked for all concerns and unaligned points before TDD refactor.
options_considered: documented preflight concerns plus test phases
decision_trail.chosen: documented preflight concerns plus test phases
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Direct implementation would encode unresolved assumptions; verbal-only concerns would not be traceable.
decision_trail.reason: The project drift is caused by unclear protocol boundaries, so the next artifact must be testable alignment.
decision_trail.impact: Phase A-F red tests should precede implementation.

decision_trail.decision: Lock the refactor around a fresh registry-first model without forced legacy compatibility.
decision_trail.context: User confirmed all eight design choices.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Legacy compatibility would preserve path-depth semantics and add unnecessary test surface.
decision_trail.reason: The product has no external users yet, so direct realignment is safer.
decision_trail.impact: TDD plan can target .fabric/rules, explicit levels, content_ref, and section-based injection directly.

decision_trail.decision: Requirement Profile will be deterministic and server-generated.
decision_trail.context: User asked how Requirement Profile should be implemented.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: LLM/AST-first approaches are harder to test and explain in the first version.
decision_trail.reason: The profile must make L1 ranking testable and explainable.
decision_trail.impact: fab_plan_context will return requirement_profile and ranked_candidates with match_reasons.

decision_trail.decision: Use unified description index with L0/L2 required and L1 AI-selectable.
decision_trail.context: User asked how to include L0/L2 while only discussing L1 AI selection.
options_considered: unified index with layer-specific selection policy
decision_trail.chosen: unified index with layer-specific selection policy
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: L1-only index hides required context; AI selecting all layers can miss L0/L2.
decision_trail.reason: AI should see the rule map but only make semantic L1 decisions.
decision_trail.impact: fab_plan_context needs required_stable_ids, ai_selectable_stable_ids, selection_policy, and selection_token.

decision_trail.decision: Use selection_token so fab_get_rule_sections server-side merges final stable ids.
decision_trail.context: Need to guarantee section fetch includes all selected and required rules.
options_considered: client passes required ids for validation
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Client-side merging is error-prone and can omit required ids.
decision_trail.reason: Server-side merge prevents omissions and enables candidate validation.
decision_trail.impact: fab_get_rule_sections input should be selection_token + ai_selected_stable_ids + sections.

decision_trail.decision: Make L1 selection reasons first-class protocol and telemetry data.
decision_trail.context: User asked whether specific L1 selection reasons are included for future measurement and improvement.
options_considered: structured protocol fields plus audit event
decision_trail.chosen: structured protocol fields plus audit event
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: No/prose-only reasons cannot be aggregated or tested.
decision_trail.reason: L1 selection quality needs an improvement loop based on evidence.
decision_trail.impact: fab_plan_context and fab_get_rule_sections schemas need reason fields and audit tests.

decision_trail.decision: Keep fab_plan_context neutral for L1 selection.
decision_trail.context: User corrected the previous proposal because server-side L1 score/reasons would bias AI judgment.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Any server-side score or reason can influence AI and reduce independent semantic judgment.
decision_trail.reason: AI should inspect files and choose L1 based on descriptions without server bias; reasons are still collected later for telemetry.
decision_trail.impact: TDD must assert plan-context omits L1 ranking fields and get-rule-sections accepts ai_selection_reasons.

decision_trail.decision: Cancel Description.id and use stable_id as the only rule identity.
decision_trail.context: User corrected prior understanding of description identity.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Description.id creates a second identity source and risks drift.
decision_trail.reason: Description is matching metadata; identity belongs to the rule node.
decision_trail.impact: RuleDescription schema should not define id; all selection/audit/section APIs use stable_id.

decision_trail.decision: Lock final TDD entry protocol details.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Open questions would make red tests unstable; deciding during implementation mixes protocol design with coding.
decision_trail.impact: Next step can generate a TDD plan directly from decision-locks, selection-token-protocol, requirement-profile-design, and tdd-entry-decisions.

follow_up_suggestions.summary: Generate TDD implementation plan from locked protocol documents.
